title: potentilla × budaiana
intro: Potentilla × budaiana, also known as Hungarian potentilla or budapest cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species in the family Rosaceae, derived from the cross between Potentilla cinerea and Potentilla crantzii. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Potentilla × budaiana include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
